---
title: "Inverse Problems: Theory, Regularization, and Bayesian Approaches"
subtitle: "Cross-Cutting Methods"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Inverse Problems: Theory, Regularization, and Bayesian Approaches

**Track**: Cross-Cutting Methods

The mathematical theory of determining causes from observed effects. Every model calibration problem is an inverse problem. This course covers the theory of ill-posedness, regularization, and modern Bayesian and computational approaches.

## Table of Contents

1. Well-Posedness and Ill-Posedness
2. Tikhonov Regularization
3. Iterative Methods for Inverse Problems
4. Bayesian Inverse Problems
5. Nonlinear Inverse Problems
6. Inverse Problems for Differential Equations
7. Sparsity and Compressed Sensing for Inverse Problems
8. Data Assimilation: Combining Models and Observations

---

## Chapter 1: Well-Posedness and Ill-Posedness

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Tikhonov Regularization

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Iterative Methods for Inverse Problems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Bayesian Inverse Problems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Nonlinear Inverse Problems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Inverse Problems for Differential Equations

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Sparsity and Compressed Sensing for Inverse Problems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Data Assimilation: Combining Models and Observations

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
